formations_blocks: Haiti one-way traffic in the latter part of the phase
key_matchups: Andy Robertson crossing for Scott McTominay
Jean-Ricner Bellegarde vs Scotland's midfield/defensive setup
phases.summary: Scotland began with more of the ball and created the first effort through Ben Gannon-Doak, while Andy Robertson and John McGinn helped set up an early chance for McTominay. Haiti then grew into the match by pressing aggressively, winning turnovers and forcing Scotland deeper, with Bellegarde emerging as their main attacking outlet from midfield and set-piece situations.

formations_blocks: Scotland controlling possession after taking the lead
Haiti defending more compactly and threatening on the break
Haiti building through to the edge of the box but struggling to find the final pass
adjustments: Gannon-Doak repeatedly cutting in from the right and creating chances from wide positions
McGinn losing the ball in midfield leading to a Haiti counterattack
Scotland managing the game more after the 28' opener while Haiti increased direct transition threat
key_matchups: Gannon-Doak vs his marker on the right flank
phases.summary: Scotland grew into the first half through right-sided attacking play, with Gannon-Doak repeatedly causing problems and McGinn eventually converting the pressure into the opening goal. After going behind, Haiti became dangerous in transition and from the left, but Scotland’s defenders and goalkeeper repeatedly intervened to protect the lead.

The half-time briefing gives no concrete tactical shape, pressing structure, or substitution information. The main takeaway is simply that Scotland lead 1-0 at the break, with the discussion focused more on the game’s entertainment value, the importance of the next goal, and general observations about Scotland coping with Haiti’s physicality.

formations_blocks: Haiti in a compact, physical low block/counter-punch setup
Scotland attacking through wide deliveries and set-piece situations
Game becoming stop-start with lots of free-kicks and little flow
adjustments: 46' Hickey booked for recovery foul on Providence after a left-side dribble
61' Haiti substitution: Deedson off, Josué Casimir on
Haiti continuing to target the left side through Providence deliveries
Scotland repeatedly using wide free-kicks/throws to sustain pressure
key_matchups: Hickey vs Providence on Haiti's left side
McGinn vs Haiti's defensive set-up on the left
phases.summary: This phase was defined by Scotland controlling territory but struggling to turn that into clear separation, while Haiti embraced a highly physical, disruptive, counter-focused approach. Haiti repeatedly threatened down their left through Providence, and after their substitution on 61' they remained dangerous enough to punish any Scottish sloppiness, including an immediate counter-threat after McGinn’s missed chance. Scotland’s best moments came from wide deliveries and set pieces, but the lack of flow and the sustained CONCACAF-style contact made the match edgy rather than settled.

formations_blocks: Scotland defending a lead in a less controlled, deeper block under Haiti pressure
Haiti pushing forward in transition and set-piece pressure, with late attacking waves
adjustments: Scotland made a triple substitution: Patterson for Hickey, Christie for Gannon-Doak, Dykes for Adams
Haiti introduced Lenny Joseph for Isidor, then Yassin Fortuné for Providence
Scotland later brought on Findlay Curtis for McGinn and Kenny McLean for Shankland
key_matchups: Pierrot vs Scotland's central defence on the right-sided cross
Joseph vs Scotland's back line immediately after coming on
McLean/Fortuné challenging Scotland in the closing free-kick situations
phases.summary: Scotland spent the final quarter under sustained Haitian pressure after leading, with Haiti generating repeated quick breaks, corners and dangerous entries into the box. The substitutions on both sides altered the forward dynamics, but the main story was Scotland trying to survive in a deeper, less composed defensive shape while Haiti pushed for an equaliser through transitions and set pieces.
